fields.description:
# Code-review near-term nit reduction: suppress docs + llm-prompts advisory noise

## Why
Code-review advisory volume is ~5 findings/review, dominated by `docs` and `llm-prompts` nits
(0/9 high-value in the dogfooded adjudication). That noise buries the findings that matter and
trains reviewers to skim. We want less nit friction on the two lowest-value advisory surfaces
without touching the blocking posture (everything ships advisory in v1).

## Scope note — the landmine detector is split out
This story ORIGINALLY also bundled a grounding-assisted "new-behavior-without-test" detector.
Investigation established that detector needs a BESPOKE seam that does not exist yet (code-review
DET detectors are Engine-B pattern rules that scan code, not diff-parse + cross-file test
correlation; `resolve.extract_references_from_diff` is imports-only, not added-definition
extraction). Building that seam is disproportionate to this near-term nit-reduction change, so it
is SPLIT OUT to the follow-up idea `acoustic-coveting-turaco` (linked `discovered_from` this
story). This story now scopes to the feasible, self-contained down-rank half only.

## What
Introduce a `nit_suppressed` routing flag on the `docs` and `llm-prompts` code-review overlays in
`src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json`, and honour it in the deterministic Pass-3
decide (`code_review_decide`): an ADVISORY finding whose criteria are ALL nit-suppressed is
DEMOTED from the surfaced advisory set to `dropped` (tagged with a reason), so it no longer adds
coaching noise. Blocking posture is untouched — the flag only affects advisory surfacing, and a
finding that ALSO maps to a non-suppressed dimension still surfaces (all-criteria rule).

## Scope / files
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` — add `"nit_suppressed": true` to the `docs`
  and `llm-prompts` entries (both stay `blocking_enabled: false`).
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py` — a `nit_suppressed_criteria(routing_map=None)` helper
  returning the set of criteria flagged `nit_suppressed` (overlay-aware, mirroring
  `threshold_for` / `effective_routing`).
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` — in `code_review_decide`, demote an advisory
  finding whose criteria are ALL nit-suppressed to `dropped`.
- `tests/unit/test_code_review_nit_routing.py` — the routing + decide-partition tests below.

No LLM-priority-threshold or blocking change here (blocking calibration is the rollout child
`raptorial-galloping-dragon`); this is advisory-surfacing only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The packaged `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` marks BOTH `docs` and
      `llm-prompts` with `"nit_suppressed": true` AND keeps them `"blocking_enabled": false`; no
      other advisory overlay (e.g. `security`, `tests`, `performance`) is `nit_suppressed`. A unit
      test asserts this over the packaged routing.
- [ ] `code_review_decide` DEMOTES an advisory finding whose criteria are ALL nit-suppressed from
      `surfaced` to `dropped`; a finding mapping to a nit-suppressed AND a non-suppressed criterion
      still surfaces; a non-suppressed advisory finding still surfaces. Unit-tested.
- [ ] The `nit_suppressed_criteria` registry helper reads the flag from the routing index
      (overlay-aware) and returns `{docs, llm-prompts}` for the packaged routing.
- [ ] Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_code_review_nit_routing.py -v`.
- [ ] The split-out landmine detector is captured as `discovered_from` follow-up
      `acoustic-coveting-turaco` (no detector code ships in this story).
